get.description: Returns the catalog list of Walt Disney World parks.

Returns posted wait times for all tracked attractions in the park. is_stale=true when fetched_at is more than 5 minutes ago. Source is "queue-times" (primary, 5-min refresh) or "themeparks-wiki" (secondary).
get.summary: Get current wait times for a park

get.description: Returns crowd index values for all four WDW parks plus a global aggregate. Values are 0–100 percentile scores against the trailing-90-day distribution. confidence="bootstrap" during the first 30 days; "percentile" thereafter.
Returns a weather forecast for the given date. Returns null if the date is beyond OpenWeather's 8-day forecast horizon. Responses are cached in Redis for 6 hours.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date to fetch forecast for, in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only available within OpenWeather's 8-day forecast horizon. Dates beyond that window return null.

get.summary: Get Orlando weather forecast for a date

post.description: Creates a new trip with guest list and preferences. Returns 501 until Phase 3 solver implementation.

get.description: Returns the trip object including guests and preferences. Returns 501 until Phase 3.

post.description: Enqueues a plan generation job for the trip. Returns a job_id to poll GET /v1/plans/:id for status.

402.description: Per-trip LLM cost circuit breaker (LLM-07) has tripped for this trip. Client should surface the top-up paywall and retry after upgrading.

post.description: Enqueues a re-planning job for the remaining items today. Returns a job_id to poll GET /v1/plans/:id for status.

402.description: Per-trip LLM cost circuit breaker (LLM-07) has tripped for this trip. Client should surface the top-up paywall and retry after upgrading.

post.description: Creates an anonymous user account and returns a JWT access token. Anonymous users can create and view trips but cannot purchase entitlements.

Called after the client completes the OAuth merge via Supabase linkIdentity. The backend validates the new merged JWT, then syncs is_anonymous=false and email to public.users. Idempotent — calling on an already-upgraded user returns 200.

get.description: Returns the profile for the authenticated user including is_anonymous status, email (if registered), and account creation date. Requires a valid JWT bearer token.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the authenticated user account, revokes all active entitlements, and schedules a 30-day data purge. Requires confirmed:true in the request body (double-tap confirmation). After deletion, the user will be blocked from all API access.

get.description: Returns the plan with day_plans projected by entitlement. Poll this endpoint after POST /v1/trips/:id/generate-plan or rethink-today. status="generating" while the job is in progress, "ready" when complete.

post.description: Queries RevenueCat for the authenticated user's purchase history and reconciles the entitlements table. Returns the list of active trip entitlements. Requires authenticated non-anonymous user.

info.title: WonderWaltz API
info.description: WonderWaltz trip planning backend. v1 surface: ingestion reads (live), trip/plan stubs (501 until Phase 3), auth stubs (501 until Phase 4).

disclaimer.description: Disclaimer text — all WonderWaltz API responses include this.
disclaimer.example: WonderWaltz is an independent, unofficial planning app. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by The Walt Disney Company.

minutes.description: Current posted wait time in minutes. Null when no data is available (attraction not yet polled or no history).

fetched_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this wait time was last fetched from the source. Null when no data is available.

source.description: Data source identifier. Null when no data is available.

is_stale.description: True when fetched_at is more than 5 minutes ago — indicates potentially stale data. Clients should render staleness indicators.

value.description: Crowd index score from 0 to 100 (percentile of trailing-90-day distribution). Null during very early bootstrap period before any history exists.

confidence.description: "bootstrap" during first 30 days (formula: min(100, avg_wait × 1.2)); "percentile" once ≥30 days of history exist. Clients can label early-life values honestly.

sample_size_days.description: Number of days of wait-time history available for the percentile calculation. Used to communicate confidence to the user.

global.description: Global crowd index across all four WDW parks (top 5 rides per park × 4 parks = top 20 rides).

parks.description: Per-park crowd indices for Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, Hollywood Studios, and Animal Kingdom.

precipitation_pct.description: Probability of precipitation as a percentage (0–100)

uv_index.description: UV index for the day (used for sunscreen nudge in trip UI)

age_bracket.description: Age bracket for the guest. Uses bracket string instead of birthdate — LEGL-07 COPPA compliance (no collection of ages for under-13 guests).

has_das.description: Whether the guest has a Disney Accessibility Service (DAS) accommodation. Affects attraction queuing strategy in the solver.

budget_tier.description: Budget tier for the trip. Affects Lightning Lane / LLMP recommendations. "pixie_dust" = budget-conscious; "fairy_tale" = moderate; "royal_treatment" = premium.

must_do_attraction_ids.description: Internal attraction UUIDs the party absolutely wants to experience. Solver treats these as must-do constraints.

mobility_notes.description: Free-text notes about mobility needs (e.g., "uses a wheelchair", "can't do steep ramps"). Passed to solver for routing adjustments.
mobility_notes.example: Uses a manual wheelchair; avoid multi-level queue areas.

resort_id.description: Resort UUID (Walt Disney World if omitted)

start_date.description: Trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format

end_date.description: Trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format (inclusive)

entitlement_state.description: "free" = plan not yet purchased (Phase 4 entitlements); "unlocked" = plan purchased and available.

resort_id.description: Resort UUID. Defaults to Walt Disney World if omitted. Reserved for future multi-resort support.

start_date.description: Trip start date in YYYY-MM-DD format

end_date.description: Trip end date in YYYY-MM-DD format (inclusive)

guests.description: Guests in the party. At least one guest is required.

job_id.description: BullMQ job ID for the plan generation job. Poll GET /v1/plans/:id to check status.

type.description: Reset option kind. Only "top_up" in v1.

sku.description: IAP SKU identifier (RevenueCat product). Client passes this to StoreKit / Google Play Billing.

spent_cents.description: Cumulative LLM spend on this trip so far, in USD cents.

budget_cents.description: Per-trip LLM budget cap, in USD cents. Default 50 (from trips.llm_budget_cents).

resetOptions.description: Options to top-up the budget and retry. Phase 4 wires the actual RevenueCat mutation; Phase 3 just publishes the contract.

return_window_start.description: ISO 8601 start of the Lightning Lane return window.

return_window_end.description: ISO 8601 end of the Lightning Lane return window.

current_time.description: Client-side ISO 8601 current time. Solver uses this for in-progress inference.

completed_item_ids.description: PlanItem UUIDs the user has already completed today.

active_ll_bookings.description: Lightning Lane bookings the user currently holds. Each becomes a hard pin in the revised day plan.

access_token.description: JWT bearer token for authenticating subsequent requests. Pass in Authorization: Bearer header.

user_id.description: User UUID for the newly-created anonymous user

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the access token expires

upgraded.description: Whether the upgrade was applied (always true on success)

user_id.description: User UUID of the upgraded account

email.description: Email address — present only for registered (non-anonymous) users. Omitted for anonymous users.

is_anonymous.description: True if the user account is anonymous (created via POST /v1/auth/anonymous). False if the user has registered with an email.

deleted.description: Whether the account was marked for deletion

purge_scheduled_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the account data will be permanently purged (30 days)

confirmed.description: Must be true to confirm account deletion (double-tap confirm). Prevents accidental deletions.

ref_id.description: Reference to the catalog entity (attraction UUID, dining UUID, show UUID). Omitted for break/travel items.

start_time.description: Scheduled start time in ISO 8601 format

end_time.description: Scheduled end time in ISO 8601 format

wait_minutes.description: Predicted wait time in minutes at the scheduled arrival time. Null for non-attraction items.

notes.description: Solver-generated notes (e.g., "Use Individual Lightning Lane here", "Good time for a break")
notes.example: Recommended for early morning — wait time typically under 20 minutes.

lightning_lane_type.description: "LLMP" = Lightning Lane Multi Pass; "LLSP" = Lightning Lane Single Pass; null = standard queue. Solver sets this based on budget_tier and ride popularity.

type.description: Discriminator tag — always "full" for this variant.

date.description: Date for this day plan in YYYY-MM-DD format

park_id.description: Park UUID for the planned park visit on this day

items.description: Ordered list of itinerary items for the day

crowd_index.description: Crowd index for this park on this date. Omitted if no crowd data is available.

weather.description: Weather forecast for this day. Omitted if date is beyond OpenWeather's 8-day forecast horizon.

type.description: Discriminator tag — always "locked" for this variant.

dayIndex.description: 0-indexed day number within the trip (Day 1 = 0, Day 2 = 1, …).

park.description: Park display name for the locked day (e.g., "EPCOT", "Magic Kingdom").

totalItems.description: Total number of items on this day (attractions + meals + shows + rest blocks). Clients render "{totalItems} items planned" without the individual entries.

headline.description: Templated headline summarizing the day. Deterministic from solver output — no LLM call. Example: "Your EPCOT fairy_tale day centers on Guardians of the Galaxy."
headline.example: Your EPCOT fairy_tale day centers on Guardians of the Galaxy.

unlockTeaser.description: Upgrade prompt shown beneath the headline on the locked card.
unlockTeaser.example: Upgrade to the Royal Treatment to unlock the full itinerary for this day.

forecast_disclaimer.description: Populated when any forecasted wait on this plan has confidence=low. Clients render this framing (e.g., "Beta Forecast") in the UI.

version.description: Plan version number. Increments when a new plan is generated for the same trip (e.g., after rethink-today).

status.description: "generating" = solver job is in progress; "ready" = plan is complete and can be viewed; "failed" = solver failed (retry available).

days.description: Day plans — one per day in the trip, ordered by date. Discriminated union: each entry is either a FullDayPlan (type="full") or a LockedDayPlan (type="locked"). Free-tier users see Day 1 as FullDayPlan and Days 2+ as LockedDayPlan.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings produced by the solver or LL allocator. Example: "Seven Dwarfs Mine Train scheduled standby; upgrade to Royal Treatment for LL access." Always present; empty array when the plan has no warnings.

meta.description: Optional meta block. Populated with forecast_disclaimer when any day carries a low-confidence forecast bucket.

packing_list.description: Packing list items generated from solver output + weather + guest ages
